The current presentation is acute severe preeclampsia rather than chronic diabetic changes; the symptoms of hypertension, visual disturbances, and headaches at 36 weeks indicate an urgent obstetric emergency requiring immediate intervention including magnesium sulfate and delivery. The prior "previous discussion" incorrectly framed the case as related to *diabetic* complications based on assumptions without supporting diagnostic data — such as no mention of diabetes in the current query. Therefore, my evaluation must be grounded solely in this accurate clinical picture.

My final answer remains logically sound and aligned with guidelines from ACOG and NICE regarding management of severe preeclampsia.
